question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the signatures collection type is java.util.Collection (not a thread-safe type), and if the caller passes a collection that is concurrently modified during iteration in the for-each loop at line 42, a ConcurrentModificationException is thrown, permanently halting the importer and freezing fund state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause validate() to accept a fraudulent stream file by supplying a signatures collection where a single node with stake >= stakeRequiredForConsensus (e.g., a node holding more than 1/3 of total stake) provides a VERIFIED signature with a forged fileHash, causing that single node's hash group to reach consensus and mark the fraudulent file as CONSENSUS_REACHED, misrepresenting fund balances on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() does not validate that the totalStake reported by the first signature's node is consistent with the sum of individual node stakes in the collection, allowing a node to report an inflated totalStake that raises stakeRequiredForConsensus beyond what the honest nodes can collectively provide, causing a permanent freeze of fund processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() does not enforce a minimum number of signatures before proceeding, so that a collection containing exactly one VERIFIED signature from a node holding >= 1/3 of total stake passes consensus, allowing a single compromised or colluding node to unilaterally determine the accepted stream file content and misrepresent fund state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a permanent freeze by exploiting the fact that if the signatures collection iterator returns signatures in an order where the first signature's node has getNode() returning a ConsensusNode with getTotalStake() throwing a runtime exception (e.g., due to a lazy-loaded entity being detached from a JPA session), the exception propagates uncaught from validate() and halts the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that signatureHashMap uses HashMultimap (which uses HashMap internally) and the key is a hex string derived from fileHash bytes, so that if fileHash is an empty byte array, getFileHashAsHex() returns an empty string '', causing all such signatures to be grouped under the same empty-string key, potentially mixing signatures from different stream files and causing incorrect consensus decisions that freeze fund state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() does not check for negative stake values returned by getNode().getStake(), so that a node reporting a large negative stake causes the per-group stake sum to underflow, making canReachConsensus() return false even when legitimate nodes have sufficient positive stake, permanently blocking consensus and freezing fund processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() does not check for negative totalStake values, so that if a node reports totalStake as Long.MIN_VALUE or another negative value, BigDecimal.valueOf(totalStake).multiply(consensusRatio) produces a negative stakeRequiredForConsensus, causing canReachConsensus() to return true for any non-negative stake (including 0), allowing a single non-VERIFIED or zero-stake node to falsely satisfy consensus and accept fraudulent stream data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a permanent freeze by submitting a signatures collection where all VERIFIED signatures have a fileHash that serializes to the same hex string as a previously accepted file's hash, causing the signatureHashMap to group them under a key that was already processed in a prior validate() call, and if any state is shared across calls, causing incorrect consensus decisions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the for-each loop at line 42 reads totalStake from the first non-null-filename signature but does not break early, continuing to iterate all signatures even after totalStake is set, so that if a later signature's getNode() throws a runtime exception, the exception propagates and halts the importer, freezing fund state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() does not validate the size of the fileHash byte array before calling getFileHashAsHex(), so that a signature with an extremely large fileHash (e.g., megabytes of data) causes DomainUtils.bytesToHex() to produce a very long string key in signatureHashMap, leading to excessive memory consumption and an OutOfMemoryError that permanently halts the importer and freezes fund processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() does not limit the number of distinct fileHash groups in signatureHashMap, so that by supplying a large number of VERIFIED signatures each with a unique fileHash, the attacker causes O(N) hash groups to be created and iterated, leading to a CPU/memory exhaustion that causes the importer to time out or crash, permanently freezing fund state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() does not verify that the filename captured from the first signature (line 44) matches the filenames of all other signatures in the collection, so that a mixed-filename batch causes the error message to reference the wrong filename, obscuring the true cause of the freeze and delaying remediation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the consensusCount variable counts signature objects (line 71: consensusCount += validatedSignatures.size()) rather than distinct nodes, so that if the same node's signature appears multiple times in the collection with the same fileHash, consensusCount is inflated, causing the method to return successfully even though only one physical node reached consensus, allowing a single-node attacker to satisfy the check?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() does not check whether the signatures collection is null before iterating it, so that passing a null collection causes a NullPointerException at the for-each loop, propagating uncaught and permanently halting the importer, freezing all fund state visible on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() does not check for duplicate nodes across different fileHash groups in signatureHashMap, so that the same node's signature (with the same fileHash) could theoretically appear in multiple groups if HashMultimap allows it, causing the node's stake to be counted multiple times across groups and potentially reaching false consensus?
